title: centaurea jacea var. nigra
intro: Centaurea jacea var. nigra, also known as the black knapweed or black hardheads, is a variety of the knapweed plant species. It is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Centaurea jacea var. nigra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
